name: responder
title: responder
description:
LLMNR/NBT-NS/mDNS Poisoner
This package contains Responder/MultiRelay,  an LLMNR, NBT-NS and MDNS
poisoner. It will answer to specific NBT-NS (NetBIOS Name Service) queries
based on their name suffix (see: http://support.microsoft.com/kb/163409). By
default, the tool will only answer to File Server Service request, which is for
SMB.
.
The concept behind this is to target your answers, and be stealthier on the
network. This also helps to ensure that you don't break legitimate NBT-NS
behavior. You can set the -r option via command line if you want to answer
to the Workstation Service request name suffix.

commands.name: responder-MultiRelay
commands.description: Warning: readline module is not available, you will not be able to use the arrow keys for command history

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use responder because LLMNR/NBT-NS/mDNS Poisoner.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use responder as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `responder [options] [args]` as documented upstream for responder.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair responder with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `responder --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

usage_sections: Install with: sudo apt install responder
